author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Landed `jjf search <query>` over the snapshot cache. Storage layer
adds `Storage::search`, `SearchHit`, `MatchedField`, and free
`make_snippet` (char-boundary safe, normalizes embedded
newlines/tabs to spaces, leading/trailing ellipsis on truncation,
`±N` char window). CLI layer adds `Commands::Search` mirroring
`Commands::Ls`'s shape: `--status`/`--label`/`--type` filters
compose AND with the substring match, `--include-comments`
toggles comments into the search set (the snapshot already
materializes them inline — no extra IO), `--limit` defaults 20
(0 = unlimited), `--snippet-context` defaults 40.

Match priority is `title > body > comments` for `matched_field`;
`score` sums occurrences across every searched field. Empty
query returns zero results (match-all is `jjf ls`'s job). JSON
envelope is `{"ok":true,"results":[{id,title,score,snippet,
matched_field}]}` — distinct from `ls`/`ready`'s bare-array
shape, as the ticket spec pinned. Plain text is silent on empty.

Smoke verification against the jjforge planner itself: `search
"concurrent_write" --include-comments --limit 5` returned 5 hits
across title/body/comments; `search "body cap" --include-comments`
returned the QA-redteam follow-up ticket; `search "" --json`
returned `{"ok":true,"results":[]}` as designed.

Workspace `cargo nextest run --workspace`: 556 / 556 passing
including 16 new storage unit tests and 8 new CLI integration
tests. Storage tests cover empty query, no match, title-only,
body-only, comments-only with/without flag, case-insensitive,
multi-field priority, snippet windowing (middle/start/end),
multibyte UTF-8 safety, newline/tab normalization. CLI tests
cover the JSON envelope, the AND filter composition, the
`--include-comments` widening, `--limit` truncation, and the
exit-2 preflight failure outside a jj repo.

`docs/cli-json.md` carries a v2.8 → v2.9 changelog block and a
new `### search` per-verb section. `CLAUDE.md`'s Queries section
gained three examples (substring, `--include-comments`,
`--json | jq` composition).

# Recommendation

Close. The acceptance list is met: id+title+snippet rows with a
default 20-row limit, `{ok:true, results:[...]}` JSON envelope,
`--include-comments` widening, AND filter composition across
`--status`/`--label`/`--type`, subsecond on the jjforge planner
(snapshot cache is already O(N) in memory), tests on every
acceptance criterion.

# Confidence

High. Storage layer is pure-data over the snapshot — no new IO
path, no new ref namespace, no spec bump. CLI mirrors `run_ls`
mechanically. Snippet windowing has explicit multibyte +
newline/tab tests. The contract is self-consistent with the rest
of `docs/cli-json.md`.

# Open follow-ups

- **Performance against the 968-issue asterinas tree.** Substring
  scan over the snapshot is O(N · L) per query (L = field length).
  Untested at that scale here — file a follow-up ticket if the
  asterinas migration measures a slowdown, then add the inverted-
  index second cache layer the ticket's Storage section sketches.
  Don't pre-build the index until benchmarks demand it.

- **Out of scope, restating so the next session doesn't get
  tempted:** regex search, fuzzy / edit-distance, BM25/TF-IDF
  relevance ranking, memories search. Memories already ship
  `jjf memories <substring>`; the others are deferred per the
  ticket body.

- **`matched_field` granularity.** Today `Comments` is the bag
  — we don't tag WHICH comment hit, just that some comment did.
  If a UX surface later wants "jump to comment N," the storage
  layer can grow a `comment_index` field on `SearchHit` without
  breaking the wire shape (additive). Not worth doing now.
